channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Trump tariffs, including massive levies on China, go into effect
item.description: President Donald Trump's latest round of sweeping reciprocal tariffs went into effect early Wednesday, including a 104 percent import tax on China.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Takeaways from AP and Lee's report on a pipeline company's lawsuits against Midwest farmers
item.description: The company behind a massive proposed carbon pipeline in the Midwestern U.S. filed hundreds of lawsuits against landowners in recent years.
item.dc:creator: Eric Ferkenhoff/Lee Enterprises And Josh Kelety/Ap, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Kennedy’s moves on measles don't quell criticism
item.description: The outbreak represents the first time Kennedy has had to reconcile his past as a leading critic of vaccines and his leadership of the federal health establishment.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More than 900 illegal aliens charged with immigration-related crimes during first week of April: DOJ
item.description: US attorneys in six districts filed immigration-related charges against nearly 1,000 illegal aliens during the first week of April under Operation Take Back America, the DOJ said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New Mexico governor mobilizes National Guard to tackle crime emergency in Albuquerque
item.description: ALBUQUERQUE, N.M. (AP) — Gov. Michelle Lujan Grisham on Tuesday declared a state of emergency in New Mexico’s largest city, saying that a significant increase in crime in Albuquerque warrants the help of the New Mexico National Guard.
item.dc:creator: SUSAN MONTOYA BRYAN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Indiana man caught raping teen near White River
item.description: According to court documents filed on Sunday in Delaware County, 30-year-old Arijan Urguni has been preliminarily charged with:
item.dc:creator: David Gay, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration halts $1 billion in federal funding for Cornell, $790 million for Northwestern
item.description: Cornell University and Northwestern University say they haven't received any notice from the U.S. government about paused federal funding even though the White House says more than $1 billion for Cornell and around $790 million for Northwestern has been frozen.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: American man on Bahamas family vacation found dead after leaving dinner to get jacket: report
item.description: A Maryland man, Dinari McAlmont, who briefly disappeared during a vacation to the Bahamas with his parents, was found dead on a beach the next morning.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: UVU Ballroom Dance Team wins first place at World Culture Dance Competition
item.description: The Utah Valley University Ballroom Dance Company Tour Team took home the first place award at the World Culture Dance Competition this last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Kayla Baggerly, ABC 4

item.title: California man angry about abortion, gun rights cases pleads guilty to trying to kill Justice Brett Kavanaugh
item.description: A California man faces up to life in prison when he's sentenced later this year for trying to assassinate U.S. Supreme Court Justice Brett Kavanaugh at his Maryland home.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Shake Shack brings Dubai Chocolate Pistachio Shake to select L.A. locations
item.description: For those who were unsuccessful in recreating the viral Dubai chocolate bar or can’t afford a trip to get an authentic bar, Shake Shack has you covered. The fast-casual chain launched its Dubai Chocolate Pistachio Shake on Monday at select locations in New York City, Los Angeles, and Miami.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: A Florida man is executed for killing a Miami Herald employee who was abducted on her lunch break
item.description: STARKE, Fla. (AP) — A Florida man convicted of killing a Miami Herald employee who was abducted on her lunch break was executed Tuesday evening. Michael Tanzi was pronounced dead at 6:12 p.m.
item.dc:creator: CURT ANDERSON, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Sonya Massey killing: Judge rules trial can proceed in Peoria County
item.description: Sean Grayson was charged with first-degree murder and fired from the sheriff’s office. He pleaded not guilty.
item.dc:creator: Danny Connolly, NewsNation

item.title: Man arrested for allegedly distributing fentanyl, meth near Jordan River Trail
item.description: A man was arrested on Monday for allegedly distributing drugs — including suspected fentanyl and methamphetamine — near the Jordan River Trail.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Keystone pipeline shut down after North Dakota rupture
item.description: The affected segment has been isolated, and operations and containment resources have been mobilized to site,” the company said.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Ex-member of former Gambia dictator's military unit is on trial in the US for torture
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A man accused of torturing people suspected in a planned coup against Gambia’s longtime leader was a low-ranking private in the West African country’s military who risked torture and death himself if he disobeyed superiors, a lawyer for the defendant told jurors Tuesday in opening stat
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democratic-led cities and states push back on threats to cut US school funding over DEI
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Some Democratic-led states and cities are pushing back on a Trump administration threat to cut education funding over diversity, equity and inclusion programs, creating a standoff that could test how far the White House is willing to go to press its demands on the nation’s schools.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Boris Johnson pecked by ostrich
item.description: Former British Prime Minister Boris Johnson had a fowl encounter with an ostrich during a sight-seeing trip in Texas.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, Fox 5

item.title: Judge denies motion to move Kouri Richins murder trial to Salt Lake County
item.description: "There's not a perfect answer here. There's not a perfect jury pool," an attorney representing Richins said.
item.dc:creator: Derick Fox, NewsNation

item.title: House Democrats targeting 3 California Republicans in 2026
item.description: The Democratic Congressional Campaign Committee rolled out a list of 35 House Republicans it plans to target in the 2026 midterms on Tuesday, including three California seats. The list, which the DCCC called its Districts in Play, includes Alaska Rep. Nick Begich (R); Arizona Reps.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KTLA 5

item.title: Spanberger raises $6.7 million in Virginia governor race
item.description: Former Rep. Abigail Spanberger’s (D-Va.) gubernatorial campaign said Monday it raised $6.7 million in the first quarter of the year. The haul included more than 50,000 donations of $100 or less in the first three months of the year, according to the Spanberger campaign.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, NewsNation

item.title: California youth soccer coach charged with murdering missing 13-year-old boy is in US illegally: sources
item.description: A California youth soccer coach charged with murdering a player who was reported missing by his family is in the U.S. illegally, law enforcement sources told Fox News.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Stephen A. Smith 'leaving all doors open' for presidential bid
item.description: Smith has a longstanding history of weighing in on politics, but has been particularly critical of the Democratic Party in the wake of President Trump’s success in the 2024 election, pushing for it to refocus on top issues for voters in last year’s election cycle including immigration and inflation.
item.dc:creator: Tara Suter, Fox 5

item.title: Florida teens beat up mall employee dressed as Easter Bunny: Police
item.description: A trio of teenagers was accused of attacking a Florida mall employee in an Easter Bunny costume on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Tucker, NewsNation

item.title: Former Sen. Joe Manchin is working on a memoir. 'Dead Center' is scheduled for September
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Former Sen. Joe Manchin has a memoir coming out in September, what the self-styled maverick from West Virginia calls his “declaration of independence from the extremes on both sides.” St.
item.dc:creator: HILLEL ITALIE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House Democrats targeting 35 Republicans in 2026
item.description: Last month, the National Republican Congressional Campaign Committee (NRCC) released its target list, which included 26 House Democrats.
item.dc:creator: Julia Manchester, KRON4

item.title: US withholding water from Mexico to address debt, official says
item.description: The United States is holding back water payments to Mexico in order to send a message, the U.S. International Boundary and Water Commissioner told Border Report.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: Utah Red Cross volunteers deployed to assist with flooding, tornado relief in several other states
item.description: Volunteers with the American Red Cross of Utah disaster team have been deployed to several states that have been affected by recent tornadoes and flooding in the Midwest and South.
item.dc:creator: Trevor Myers, ABC 4

item.title: Swollen rivers are flooding towns in the US South after a prolonged deluge of rain
item.description: FRANKFORT, Ky. (AP) — Days of unrelenting downpours swelled rivers to near record levels across Kentucky on Monday, submerging neighborhoods and threatening a famed bourbon distillery in the state capital.
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and KRISTIN M. HALL, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: China threatens countermeasures after Trump threatens additional 50% tariff
item.description: China threatened Tuesday to take countermeasures against the United States "to safeguard its own rights and interests” in response to President Donald Trump’s threat of an additional 50% tariff on Chinese imports.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Idaho mom who killed 2 of her kids goes on trial over husband's death
item.description: Lori Vallow Daybell, the Idaho mother with doomsday religious beliefs who was convicted of killing her two youngest children and conspiring to murder a romantic rival, is on trial again. This time, she's accused in Arizona of conspiring to murder her estranged husband.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Epic Universe opens a portal to the future of theme parks
item.description: As Universal Orlando Resort gears up for the highly anticipated May 22 opening of Epic Universe, it’s clear the resort is entering a new era - one that could redefine what a theme park can offer families.
item.dc:creator: Scott Gustin, KRON4

item.title: Reversible damage found in marathon runners' brains: study
item.description: A 26.2-mile run can do some damage to the brain, a study shows, but the impact is temporary and reversible.
item.dc:creator: Ashley N. Soriano, Fox 5

item.title: Trump planning military parade through DC for 79th birthday
item.description: The Trump administration is planning a June 14 military parade through the streets of Washington, D.C., to mark the president's 79th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, Fox 5

item.title: VIDEO: 6-year-old found wandering alone with dog in Florida; mom, 1 other charged
item.description: The Volusia Sheriff's Office said a 6-year-old girl and her dog were found wandering alone on Friday afternoon in Deltona.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, ABC 4

item.title: Mega Millions tickets rise to $5, but the lottery promises more giant jackpots
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Mega Millions players will get slightly better odds and should start seeing more billion-dollar jackpots, but at a cost — literally — with tickets for the multistate lottery jumping in price to $5.
item.dc:creator: SCOTT McFETRIDGE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump threatens additional 50 percent tariff on China
item.description: The White House had already imposed a 20 percent tariff on Chinese imports over fentanyl concerns, meaning China could face an effective tariff rate as high as 70 percent.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, Fox 5
